conventions_srcmiddleware_606961868941.content: Middleware factories (create*Middleware) attach the underlying limiter instance as a property on the returned function (middleware.limiter = limiter) so tests can call mw.limiter.destroy() for cleanup

conventions_srcmiddleware_606963737253.content: Tests always use afterEach with vi.useRealTimers() to restore real timers after fake-timer tests, and call limiter?.destroy() on the instance under test.

conventions_srcmiddleware_606965265523.content: Fake timers (vi.useFakeTimers()) are called inside individual tests, not in a beforeEach — each test manages its own timer state independently.

thoughts_srcmiddlewareratelimitts_606966.content: cleanupTimer.unref() must be called on setInterval cleanup timers so the Node process can exit even with an active interval.

facts_srcmiddlewareratelimitts_606968132.content: New SimpleRateLimiter reuses the existing byIp helper from rate-limiter.ts via import rather than duplicating x-forwarded-for parsing logic.

thoughts_srcmiddlewareratelimitts_782906.content: X-RateLimit-Reset header used retryAfterMs (relative duration) as an absolute timestamp; must divide by 1000 for seconds and add to Date.now()

conventions_srcmiddleware_1044636762050.content: Middleware files live in src/middleware/ and co-located tests use the .test.ts suffix in the same directory.

conventions_srcmiddleware_1044638142594.content: Follow the factory pattern: createXxxMiddleware(config) returning a NextResponse | undefined, with destroy() on the limiter for cleanup.

facts_srcmiddlewareratelimitts_104463949.content: When adding a new file that overlaps with an existing one (rate-limiter vs rateLimit), name it distinctly and mirror the existing patterns to avoid reviewers second-guessing intent.

thoughts_srcmiddlewareratelimitts_107949.content: IP blacklist/whitelist precedence: check blacklist BEFORE whitelist, not after. Existing rate-limiter.ts does this correctly, new code inverted the order.

conventions_srcmiddlewareratelimiterts_1.content: Middleware factory pattern (create*Middleware) + limiter class with destroy() cleanup is the established convention in this codebase.

facts_srcmiddlewareratelimitts_107950377.content: X-RateLimit-Reset should be Unix seconds (integer), not milliseconds. Client-side Retry-After parsing depends on this.

conventions_srcmiddleware_1079505245676.content: Tests co-located with source (rateLimit.test.ts alongside rateLimit.ts), using vi.useRealTimers() in afterEach and NextRequest factory helper.

thoughts_srcmiddleware_1472092678283.content: Import path for the new middleware file must be correct — ensure the module resolves without conflicts with similarly named files in src/middleware.

thoughts_srcmiddlewareratelimitts_147209.content: The X-RateLimit-Reset header timestamp must be set correctly; this bug was caught in a previous review pass and fixed — watch for it.

conventions_srcmiddleware_1472095853795.content: Match existing middleware file naming: use kebab-case (rate-limiter.ts) rather than camelCase (rateLimit.ts) to stay consistent with the codebase.

conventions_testing_1472097497209.content: Co-locate Vitest tests next to source (rateLimit.test.ts beside rateLimit.ts) and mirror the established test patterns: describe/it/expect/vi, afterEach with vi.useRealTimers(), and .destroy() cleanup

facts_middleware_1472098843591.content: When reusing patterns from an existing implementation (e.g. factory pattern, SlidingWindowRateLimiter), extract the structural template (create*Middleware factory, cleanup timer, destroy method) rathe

thoughts_srcmiddlewareratelimitts_151226.content: X-RateLimit-Reset header uses `(Date.now() + retryAfterMs) / 1000` which computes an absolute Unix timestamp that drifts — use a stable reset time computed at response time, not derived from Date.now(

facts_srcmiddlewareratelimittestts_15122.content: Always verify newly exported helpers (like `byApiKey`) are covered by tests before merging; untested exports are easy to miss in diff review.

conventions_srcmiddleware_1512271566439.content: Middleware uses a factory pattern: `createXxxMiddleware()` returning a handler, with a paired `XxxConfig` interface and X-RateLimit-* + 429+Retry-After response shape.

conventions_testts_1512273575193.content: Test files use `describe/it/expect/vi`, `afterEach` with `vi.useRealTimers()`, a `NextRequest` factory helper, and call `limiter.destroy()` for cleanup.
